subject: Re: gain correction and 3d classification
body:
Hi Amin,

1. After completing all the refinements in M, is there any way to bring all
> the particles back into Relion for 3D classification? what would be the
> best approach for that?


This is planned but I've had to park it for now so no ETA: here is the
issue to track: https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/149
The interim solution is to modify the STAR files from M and export using
WarpTools ts_export_particles, I haven't done this myself so don't have a
protocol/script for you.

2. I was double-checking the gain correction to ensure it's correct for our
> setup. I estimated the gain by summing up all the TIFF files and checking
> the output (I've attached the .py file, which is not inverted). When I
> compared the achieved result (mygain.mrc) with the gain.mrc from the
> apoferritin data, they were identical. My question is: Why do we need to
> flip the data using --gain_flip_y?
>

I took the advice from the EMPIAR entry
<https://www.ebi.ac.uk/empiar/EMPIAR-10491/> about flipping the gain for
this dataset and didn't actively check it - it's possible that it's wrong.

Cheers,

Alister



On Mon, 23 Sept 2024 at 06:51, Ameen Khosrozadeh <m.a.khosrozadeh@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Alister,
>
> Thank you for developing such a great tool!  have a couple of questions
> that I’d really appreciate your help with:
>
> 1. After completing all the refinements in M, is there any way to bring
> all the particles back into Relion for 3D classification? what would be the
> best approach for that?
>
> 2. I was double-checking the gain correction to ensure it's correct for
> our setup. I estimated the gain by summing up all the TIFF files and
> checking the output (I've attached the .py file, which is not inverted).
> When I compared the achieved result (mygain.mrc) with the gain.mrc from the
> apoferritin data, they were identical. My question is: Why do we need to
> flip the data using --gain_flip_y?
>
> (cause when I estimate the gain in the same way for our Falcon 4i detector
> (data acquired with Tomo5), the result is flipped in both the X and Y axes
> compared to the microscope output)
>
> I appreciate your guidance and help.
>
> Best regards,
> Amin
>
